member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that compiler support for a particular feature is required for the location where this attribute is applied.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initialize a new instance of

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.FeatureName
member.summary: The name of the compiler feature.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.IsOptional
member.summary: If true, the compiler can choose to allow access to the location where this attribute is applied if it does not understand .

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RefStructs
member.summary: The used for the ref structs C# feature.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RequiredMembers
member.summary: The used for the required members C# feature.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DisableRuntimeMarshallingAttribute
member.summary: Disables the built-in runtime managed/unmanaged marshalling subsystem for P/Invokes, Delegate types, and unmanaged function pointer invocations.
member.remarks:
The built-in marshalling subsystem has some behaviors that cannot be changed due to backward-compatibility requirements. This attribute allows disabling the built-in subsystem and instead uses the following rules for P/Invokes, Delegates, and unmanaged function pointer invocations:
- All value types that do not contain reference type fields recursively (unmanaged in C#) are blittable - Value types that recursively have any fields that have [StructLayout(LayoutKind.Auto)] are disallowed from interop. - All reference types are disallowed from usage in interop scenarios. - SetLastError support in P/Invokes is disabled. - varargs support is disabled. - LCIDConversionAttribute support is disabled.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute
member.summary: Indicates which arguments to a method involving an interpolated string handler should be passed to that handler.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the argument that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The names of the arguments that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.Arguments
member.summary: Gets the names of the arguments that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the attributed type is to be used as an interpolated string handler.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ModuleInitializerAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that a method should be called in its containing module's initializer.
member.remarks:
When one or more valid methods with this attribute are found in a compilation, the compiler will emit a module initializer which calls each of the attributed methods.
Certain requirements are imposed on any method targeted with this attribute: - The method must be `static`. - The method must be an ordinary member method, as opposed to a property accessor, constructor, local function, etc. - The method must be parameterless. - The method must return `void`. - The method must not be generic or be contained in a generic type. - The method's effective accessibility must be `internal` or `public`.
The specification for module initializers in the .NET runtime can be found here: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/master/docs/design/specs/Ecma-335-Augments.md#module-initializer

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiredMemberAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a type has required members or that a member is required.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.SkipLocalsInitAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that the .locals init flag should not be set in method headers.
member.remarks: This attribute is unsafe because it may reveal uninitialized memory to the application in certain instances (e.g., reading from uninitialized stackalloc'd memory). If applied to a method directly, the attribute applies to that method and all nested functions (lambdas, local functions) below it. If applied to a type or module, it applies to all methods nested inside. This attribute is intentionally not permitted on assemblies. Use at the module level instead to apply to multiple type declarations.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.OSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Base type for all platform-specific API attributes.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.ObsoletedOSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Marks APIs that were obsoleted in a given operating system version.
member.remarks: Primarily used by OS bindings to indicate APIs that should not be used anymore.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.SupportedOSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Records the operating system (and minimum version) that supports an API. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate support on multiple operating systems.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a or use guards to prevent calls to APIs on unsupported operating systems.
A given platform should only be specified once.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.SupportedOSPlatformGuardAttribute
member.summary: Annotates a custom guard field, property or method with a supported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple supported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements in order to safely call platform specific APIs.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.TargetPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Records the platform that the project targeted.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.UnsupportedOSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Marks APIs that were removed in a given operating system version.
member.remarks: Primarily used by OS bindings to indicate APIs that are only available in earlier versions.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.UnsupportedOSPlatformGuardAttribute
member.summary: Annotates the custom guard field, property or method with an unsupported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple unsupported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements as a guard to safely call APIs unsupported on those platforms.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.InteropServices.SuppressGCTransitionAttribute
member.summary: An attribute used to indicate a GC transition should be skipped when making an unmanaged function call.
member.remarks:
This attribute is ignored if applied to a method without the .
Forgoing this transition can yield benefits when the cost of the transition is more than the execution time of the unmanaged function. However, avoiding this transition removes some of the guarantees the runtime provides through a normal P/Invoke. When exiting the managed runtime to enter an unmanaged function the GC must transition from Cooperative mode into Preemptive mode. Full details on these modes can be found at https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/master/docs/coding-guidelines/clr-code-guide.md#2.1.8. Suppressing the GC transition is an advanced scenario and should not be done without fully understanding potential consequences.
One of these consequences is an impact to Mixed-mode debugging (https://docs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/debugger/how-to-debug-in-mixed-mode). During Mixed-mode debugging, it is not possible to step into or set breakpoints in a P/Invoke that has been marked with this attribute. A workaround is to switch to native debugging and set a breakpoint in the native function. In general, usage of this attribute is not recommended if debugging the P/Invoke is important, for example stepping through the native code or diagnosing an exception thrown from the native code.
The runtime may load the native library for method marked with this attribute in advance before the method is called for the first time. Usage of this attribute is not recommended for platform neutral libraries with conditional platform specific code.
The P/Invoke method that this attribute is applied to must have all of the following properties: * Native function always executes for a trivial amount of time (less than 1 microsecond). * Native function does not perform a blocking syscall (e.g. any type of I/O). * Native function does not call back into the runtime (e.g. Reverse P/Invoke). * Native function does not throw exceptions. * Native function does not manipulate locks or other concurrency primitives.
Consequences of invalid uses of this attribute: * GC starvation. * Immediate runtime termination. * Data corruption.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute
member.summary: Any method marked with can be directly called from native code. The function token can be loaded to a local variable using the address-of operator in C# and passed as a callback to a native method.
member.remarks: Methods marked with this attribute have the following restrictions: * Method must be marked "static". * Must not be called from managed code. * Must only have blittable arguments.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute.CallConvs
member.summary: Optional. If omitted, the runtime will use the default platform calling convention.
member.remarks: Supplied types must be from the official "System.Runtime.CompilerServices" namespace and be of the form "CallConvXXX".

member.name: F:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute.EntryPoint
member.summary: Optional. If omitted, no named export is emitted during compilation.

member.name: T:System.Index
member.summary: Represent a type can be used to index a collection either from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Construct an Index using a value and indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.param: The index value. it has to be zero or positive number.
Indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.remarks: If the Index constructed from the end, index value 1 means pointing at the last element and index value 0 means pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: P:System.Index.Start
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at first element.

member.name: P:System.Index.End
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromStart(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the start at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the start.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromEnd(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the end at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the end.

member.name: P:System.Index.Value
member.summary: Returns the index value.

member.name: P:System.Index.IsFromEnd
member.summary: Indicates whether the index is from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.GetOffset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the offset from the start using the giving collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the Index will be used with. length has to be a positive value
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter and the returned offset value against negative values. we don't validate either the returned offset is greater than the input length. It is expected Index will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. If the returned offset is negative and then used to index a collection will get out of range exception which will be same affect as the validation.

member.name: M:System.Index.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Indicates whether the current Index object is equal to another object of the same type.
member.param: An object to compare with this object

member.name: M:System.Index.Equals(System.Index)
member.summary: Indicates whether the current Index object is equal to another Index object.
member.param: An object to compare with this object

member.name: M:System.Index.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.

member.name: M:System.Index.op_Implicit(System.Int32)~System.Index
member.summary: Converts integer number to an Index.

member.name: M:System.Index.ToString
member.summary: Converts the value of the current Index object to its equivalent string representation.

member.name: T:System.Range
member.summary: Represent a range has start and end indexes.

member.name: M:System.Range.#ctor(System.Index,System.Index)
member.summary: Represent a range has start and end indexes.

member.name: M:System.Range.ToString
member.summary: Converts the value of the current Range object to its equivalent string representation.

member.name: M:System.Range.StartAt(System.Index)
member.summary: Create a Range object starting from start index to the end of the collection.

member.name: M:System.Range.EndAt(System.Index)
member.summary: Create a Range object starting from first element in the collection to the end Index.

member.name: P:System.Range.All
member.summary: Create a Range object starting from first element to the end.

member.name: M:System.Range.GetOffsetAndLength(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the start offset and length of range object using a collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the range will be used with. length has to be a positive value.
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter against negative values. It is expected Range will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. We validate the range is inside the length scope though.

member.name: T:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfo
member.summary: A class that represents nullability info

member.name: P:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfo.Type
member.summary: The of the member or generic parameter to which this NullabilityInfo belongs

member.name: P:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfo.ReadState
member.summary: The nullability read state of the member

member.name: P:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfo.WriteState
member.summary: The nullability write state of the member

member.name: P:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfo.ElementType
member.summary: If the member type is an array, gives the of the elements of the array, null otherwise

member.name: P:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfo.GenericTypeArguments
member.summary: If the member type is a generic type, gives the array of for each type parameter

member.name: T:System.Reflection.NullabilityState
member.summary: An enum that represents nullability state

member.name: F:System.Reflection.NullabilityState.Unknown
member.summary: Nullability context not enabled (oblivious)

member.name: F:System.Reflection.NullabilityState.NotNull
member.summary: Non nullable value or reference type

member.name: F:System.Reflection.NullabilityState.Nullable
member.summary: Nullable value or reference type

member.name: T:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfoContext
member.summary: Provides APIs for populating nullability information/context from reflection members: , , and .

member.name: M:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfoContext.Create(System.Reflection.ParameterInfo)
member.summary: Populates for the given . If the nullablePublicOnly feature is set for an assembly, like it does in .NET SDK, the private and/or internal member's nullability attributes are omitted, in this case the API will return NullabilityState.Unknown state.
member.param: The parameter which nullability info gets populated
member.exception: If the parameterInfo parameter is null

member.name: M:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfoContext.Create(System.Reflection.PropertyInfo)
member.summary: Populates for the given . If the nullablePublicOnly feature is set for an assembly, like it does in .NET SDK, the private and/or internal member's nullability attributes are omitted, in this case the API will return NullabilityState.Unknown state.
member.param: The parameter which nullability info gets populated
member.exception: If the propertyInfo parameter is null

member.name: M:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfoContext.Create(System.Reflection.EventInfo)
member.summary: Populates for the given . If the nullablePublicOnly feature is set for an assembly, like it does in .NET SDK, the private and/or internal member's nullability attributes are omitted, in this case the API will return NullabilityState.Unknown state.
member.param: The parameter which nullability info gets populated
member.exception: If the eventInfo parameter is null

member.name: M:System.Reflection.NullabilityInfoContext.Create(System.Reflection.FieldInfo)
member.summary: Populates for the given If the nullablePublicOnly feature is set for an assembly, like it does in .NET SDK, the private and/or internal member's nullability attributes are omitted, in this case the API will return NullabilityState.Unknown state.
member.param: The parameter which nullability info gets populated
member.exception: If the fieldInfo parameter is null

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.AllowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that is allowed as an input even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DisallowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that is disallowed as an input even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a method that will never return under any circumstance.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method will not return if the associated parameter is passed the specified value.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.ParameterValue
member.summary: Gets the condition parameter value. Code after the method is considered unreachable by diagnostics if the argument to the associated parameter matches this value.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified parameter value.
member.param: The condition parameter value. Code after the method is considered unreachable by diagnostics if the argument to the associated parameter matches this value.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output may be even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter may be even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter may be .

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter may be .

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not- values.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with a field or property member.
member.param: The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the list of field and property members.
member.param: The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have non- values when returning with the specified return value condition.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and a field or property member.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be .
The field or property member that is promised to be not-.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and list of field and property members.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be .
The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output is not even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the associated parameter name. The output will be non- if the argument to the parameter specified is non-.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the associated parameter name.
member.param: The associated parameter name. The output will be non- if the argument to the parameter specified is non-.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter will not be even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be .

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be .

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.SetsRequiredMembersAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that this constructor sets all required members for the current type, and callers do not need to set any required members themselves.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the syntax used in a string.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.
member.param: The syntax identifier.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.
member.param: The syntax identifier.
Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Syntax
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the syntax used.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Arguments
member.summary: Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.CompositeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing composite formats for string formatting.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateTimeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date and time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.EnumFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.GuidFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Json
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing JavaScript Object Notation (JSON).

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.NumericFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing numeric format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Regex
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing regular expressions.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeSpanFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Uri
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing URIs.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Xml
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing XML.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute
member.summary: States a dependency that one member has on another.
member.remarks: This can be used to inform tooling of a dependency that is otherwise not evident purely from metadata and IL, for example a member relied on via reflection.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on the same type as the consumer.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a .
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The containing .

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a type in an assembly.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The full name of the type containing the specified member.
The assembly name of the type containing the specified member.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified types of members on a .
member.param: The types of members depended on.
The containing the specified members.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified types of members on a type in an assembly.
member.param: The types of members depended on.
The full name of the type containing the specified members.
The assembly name of the type containing the specified members.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberSignature
member.summary: Gets the signature of the member depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Type
member.summary: Gets the containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.TypeName
member.summary: Gets the full name of the type containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.AssemblyName
member.summary: Gets the assembly name of the specified type.
member.remarks: is only valid when is specified.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Condition
member.summary: Gets or sets the condition in which the dependency is applicable, e.g. "DEBUG".

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes
member.summary:
Specifies the types of members that are dynamically accessed.
This enumeration has a attribute that allows a bitwise combination of its member values.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.None
member.summary: Specifies no members.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicParameterlessConstructor
member.summary: Specifies the default, parameterless public constructor.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all non-public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all non-public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all non-public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all non-public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all non-public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all non-public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.Interfaces
member.summary: Specifies all interfaces implemented by the type.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All
member.summary: Specifies all members.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified member types.
member.param: The types of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.StackTraceHiddenAttribute
member.summary: Types and Methods attributed with StackTraceHidden will be omitted from the stack trace text shown in StackTrace.ToString() and Exception.StackTrace

member.name: T:PolyfillExtensions
member.summary: Static and thread safe wrapper around NullabilityInfoContext.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetStreamAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a GET request to the specified Uri and return the response body as a stream in an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: This operation will not block. The returned object will complete after the response headers are read. This method does not read nor buffer the response body.
member.param: The Uri the request is sent to.
The cancellation token to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetStreamAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.Uri,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a GET request to the specified Uri and return the response body as a stream in an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: This operation will not block. The returned object will complete after the response headers are read. This method does not read nor buffer the response body.
member.param: The Uri the request is sent to.
The cancellation token to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetByteArrayAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a GET request to the specified Uri and return the response body as a byte array in an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: This operation will not block. The returned object will complete after the response headers are read. This method does not read nor buffer the response body.
member.param: The Uri the request is sent to.
The cancellation token to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetByteArrayAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.Uri,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a GET request to the specified Uri and return the response body as a byte array in an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: This operation will not block. The returned object will complete after the response headers are read. This method does not read nor buffer the response body.
member.param: The Uri the request is sent to.
The cancellation token to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetStringAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a GET request to the specified Uri and return the response body as a string in an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: This operation will not block. The returned object will complete after the response headers are read. This method does not read nor buffer the response body.
member.param: The Uri the request is sent to.
The cancellation token to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetStringAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.Uri,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a GET request to the specified Uri and return the response body as a string in an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: This operation will not block. The returned object will complete after the response headers are read. This method does not read nor buffer the response body.
member.param: The Uri the request is sent to.
The cancellation token to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.ReadAsStreamAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpContent,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Serializes the HTTP content and returns a stream that represents the content.
member.remarks: Note that this method will internally buffer the content unless CreateContentReadStreamAsync() has been implemented to do otherwise.
member.param: The token to monitor for cancellation requests. The default value is .
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.ReadAsByteArrayAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpContent,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Serializes the HTTP content to a byte array as an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: Note that this method will internally buffer the content unless CreateContentReadStreamAsync() has been implemented to do otherwise.
member.param: The token to monitor for cancellation requests. The default value is .
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.ReadAsStringAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpContent,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Serializes the HTTP content to a string as an asynchronous operation.
member.remarks: Note that this method will internally buffer the content unless CreateContentReadStreamAsync() has been implemented to do otherwise.
member.param: The token to monitor for cancellation requests. The default value is .
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.MaxBy``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Func{``0,``1})
member.summary: Returns the maximum value in a generic sequence according to a specified key selector function.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements of .
The type of key to compare elements by.
member.param: A sequence of values to determine the maximum value of.
A function to extract the key for each element.
member.returns: The value with the maximum key in the sequence.
member.exception: No key extracted from implements the or interface.

remarks.para: If is a reference type and the source sequence is empty or contains only values that are , this method returns .

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.MaxBy``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Func{``0,``1},System.Collections.Generic.IComparer{``1})
member.summary: Returns the maximum value in a generic sequence according to a specified key selector function.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements of .
The type of key to compare elements by.
member.param: A sequence of values to determine the maximum value of.
A function to extract the key for each element.
The to compare keys.
member.returns: The value with the maximum key in the sequence.
member.exception: No key extracted from implements the or interface.

remarks.para: If is a reference type and the source sequence is empty or contains only values that are , this method returns .

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.MinBy``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Func{``0,``1})
member.summary: Returns the minimum value in a generic sequence according to a specified key selector function.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements of .
The type of key to compare elements by.
member.param: A sequence of values to determine the minby value of.
A function to extract the key for each element.
member.returns: The value with the minimum key in the sequence.
member.exception: No key extracted from implements the or interface.

remarks.para: If is a reference type and the source sequence is empty or contains only values that are , this method returns .

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.MinBy``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Func{``0,``1},System.Collections.Generic.IComparer{``1})
member.summary: Returns the minimum value in a generic sequence according to a specified key selector function.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements of .
The type of key to compare elements by.
member.param: A sequence of values to determine the minimum value of.
A function to extract the key for each element.
The to compare keys.
member.returns: The value with the minimum key in the sequence.
member.exception: No key extracted from implements the or interface.

remarks.para: If is a reference type and the source sequence is empty or contains only values that are , this method returns .

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.SkipLast``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a new enumerable collection that contains the elements from source with the last count elements of the source collection omitted.
member.param: An enumerable collection instance.
The number of elements to omit from the end of the collection.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the enumerable collection.
member.returns: A new enumerable collection that contains the elements from source minus count elements from the end of the collection.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetValueOrDefault``2(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{``0,``1},``0)
member.summary: Tries to get the value associated with the specified key in the dictionary.
member.param: A dictionary with keys of type TKey and values of type TValue.
The key of the value to get.
member.typeparam: The type of the keys in the dictionary.
The type of the values in the dictionary.
member.returns: A TValue instance. When the method is successful, the returned object is the value associated with the specified key. When the method fails, it returns the default value for TValue.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetValueOrDefault``2(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{``0,``1},``0,``1)
member.summary: Tries to get the value associated with the specified key in the dictionary.
member.param: A dictionary with keys of type TKey and values of type TValue.
The key of the value to get.
A dictionary with keys of type TKey and values of type TValue.
member.typeparam: The type of the keys in the dictionary.
The type of the values in the dictionary.
member.returns: A TValue instance. When the method is successful, the returned object is the value associated with the specified key. When the method fails, it returns the default value for TValue.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Nanoseconds(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Gets the nanosecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Nanosecond(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Gets the nanosecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Nanosecond(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Gets the nanosecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Microseconds(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Gets the microsecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Microsecond(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Gets the microsecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Microsecond(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Gets the microsecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.AddMicroseconds(System.DateTime,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a new object that adds a specified number of microseconds to the value of this instance..

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.AddMicroseconds(System.DateTimeOffset,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a new object that adds a specified number of microseconds to the value of this instance..

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetHashCode(System.String,System.StringComparison)
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this string using the specified rules.
member.param: One of the enumeration values that specifies the rules to use in the comparison.
member.returns: A 32-bit signed integer hash code.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Contains(System.String,System.String,System.StringComparison)
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether a specified string occurs within this string, using the specified comparison rules.
member.param: The string to seek.
One of the enumeration values that specifies the rules to use in the comparison.
member.returns: true if the value parameter occurs within this string, or if value is the empty string (""); otherwise, false.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.StartsWith(System.String,System.Char)
member.summary: Determines whether this string instance starts with the specified character.
member.param: The character to compare.
member.remarks: This method performs an ordinal (case-sensitive and culture-insensitive) comparison.
member.returns: true if value matches the beginning of this string; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.EndsWith(System.String,System.Char)
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether a specified character occurs within this string.
member.param: The character to seek.
member.remarks: This method performs an ordinal (case-sensitive and culture-insensitive) comparison.
member.returns: true if the value parameter occurs within this string; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Split(System.String,System.Char,System.StringSplitOptions)
member.summary: Splits a string into a maximum number of substrings based on a specified delimiting character and, optionally, options. Splits a string into a maximum number of substrings based on the provided character separator, optionally omitting empty substrings from the result.
member.param: A character that delimits the substrings in this instance.
A bitwise combination of the enumeration values that specifies whether to trim substrings and include empty substrings.
member.returns: An array that contains at most count substrings from this instance that are delimited by separator.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Split(System.String,System.Char,System.Int32,System.StringSplitOptions)
member.summary: Splits a string into a maximum number of substrings based on a specified delimiting character and, optionally, options. Splits a string into a maximum number of substrings based on the provided character separator, optionally omitting empty substrings from the result.
member.param: A character that delimits the substrings in this instance.
The maximum number of elements expected in the array.
A bitwise combination of the enumeration values that specifies whether to trim substrings and include empty substrings.
member.returns: An array that contains at most count substrings from this instance that are delimited by separator.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Contains(System.String,System.Char)
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether a specified character occurs within this string.
member.remarks: This method performs an ordinal (case-sensitive and culture-insensitive) comparison.
member.param: The character to seek.
member.returns: true if the value parameter occurs within this string; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.ReadToEndAsync(System.IO.TextReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads all characters from the current position to the end of the stream asynchronously and returns them as one string.
member.param: The token to monitor for cancellation requests.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous read operation. The value of the TResult parameter contains a string with the characters from the current position to the end of the stream.
member.exception: The number of characters is larger than .
The stream reader has been disposed.
The reader is currently in use by a previous read operation.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.IsGenericMethodParameter(System.Type)
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the current Type represents a type parameter in the definition of a generic method.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.GetMemberWithSameMetadataDefinitionAs(System.Type,System.Reflection.MemberInfo)
member.summary: Searches for the MemberInfo on the current Type that matches the specified MemberInfo.
member.param: The MemberInfo to find on the current Type.
The MemberInfo to find on the current Type.
member.returns: An object representing the member on the current Type that matches the specified member.

member.name: T:Destructurama.Attributed.IPropertyDestructuringAttribute
member.summary: Base interfaces for all s that override how a property is destructured.

member.name: M:Destructurama.Attributed.IPropertyDestructuringAttribute.TryCreateLogEventProperty(System.String,System.Object,Serilog.Core.ILogEventPropertyValueFactory,Serilog.Events.LogEventProperty@)
member.summary: Attempt to create a replacement for a property.
member.param: The current property name.
The current property value
The current .
The to use as a replacement.
member.returns: trueIf a replacement has been derived.

member.name: T:Destructurama.Attributed.ITypeDestructuringAttribute
member.summary: Base interfaces for all s that override how a property type type is destructured.

member.name: M:Destructurama.Attributed.ITypeDestructuringAttribute.CreateLogEventPropertyValue(System.Object,Serilog.Core.ILogEventPropertyValueFactory)
member.summary: Attempt to create a replacement for a property.
member.param: The value of the property.
The current .
member.returns: The new to use when logging the property.

member.name: T:Destructurama.Attributed.LogAsScalarAttribute
member.summary: Specified that the type or property it is applied to should never be destructured; instead it should be logged as an atomic value.

member.name: M:Destructurama.Attributed.LogAsScalarAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Construct a .
member.param: Whether the scalar value should be converted into a string before being passed down the (asynchronous) logging pipeline. For mutable types, specify true, otherwise leave as false.

member.name: T:Destructurama.Attributed.LogMaskedAttribute
member.summary: Apply to a property to apply a mask to the logged value.

member.name: P:Destructurama.Attributed.LogMaskedAttribute.Text
member.summary: If set, the property value will be set to this text.

member.name: P:Destructurama.Attributed.LogMaskedAttribute.ShowFirst
member.summary: Shows the first x characters in the property value.

member.name: P:Destructurama.Attributed.LogMaskedAttribute.ShowLast
member.summary: Shows the last x characters in the property value.

member.name: P:Destructurama.Attributed.LogMaskedAttribute.PreserveLength
member.summary: If set, it will swap out each character with the default value. Note that this property will be ignored if has been set to custom value.

member.name: T:Destructurama.Attributed.LogReplacedAttribute
member.summary: Apply to a property to use a replace the current value.

member.name: P:Destructurama.Attributed.LogReplacedAttribute.Options
member.summary: The RegexOptions that will be applied. Defaults to

member.name: P:Destructurama.Attributed.LogReplacedAttribute.Timeout
member.summary: A time-out interval to evaluate regular expression. Defaults to

member.name: M:Destructurama.Attributed.LogReplacedAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Construct a .
member.param: The pattern that should be applied on value.
The pattern that should be applied on value.

member.name: T:Destructurama.Attributed.LogWithNameAttribute
member.summary: Apply to a property to use a custom name when that property is logged.

member.name: M:Destructurama.Attributed.LogWithNameAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Construct a .
member.param: The new name to use when logging the target property.

member.name: T:Destructurama.Attributed.NotLoggedAttribute
member.summary: Specified that a property should not be included when destructuring an object for logging.

member.name: T:Destructurama.LoggerConfigurationAppSettingsExtensions
member.summary: Adds the Destructure.UsingAttributes() extension to .

member.name: M:Destructurama.LoggerConfigurationAppSettingsExtensions.UsingAttributes(Serilog.Configuration.LoggerDestructuringConfiguration)
member.summary: Adds a custom to enable manipulation of how objects are logged to Serilog using attributes.
member.param: The logger configuration to apply configuration to.
member.returns: An object allowing configuration to continue.
